section: ABSTRACT
text: Breeding is expected to become a necessity in about 40 years and in view of the 20 years development time and 30 years service life of breeder reactors, development of such reactors at present is timely.

section: ABSTRACT
text: This seems to prevent such breeders from furnishing a large fraction of the energy demands of the expanding economy from uranium recoverable at or about present cost. $\mathrm{U}^{233}$ breeders can be designed to the requirements of low inventory and short doubling time, but the aqueous homogeneous reactor seems to be the only type which can adequately meet these requirements.

section: I. HISTORY AND SCOPE OF THE STUDY
text: The Oak Ridge National Laboratory has conducted a study regarding breeding on the Th $^{232}$ -U $^{233}$ cycle.

section: I. HISTORY AND SCOPE OF THE STUDY
text: Object of the study was, on one hand, the importance of breeding on this cycle and, on the other, a comparison of the various reactor types with respect to their suitability as U $^{233}$ breeders.

section: III. COMPARISON OF PLUTONIUM AND U233 BREEDING
text: From a practical viewpoint, the main difference between plutonium and $U^{233}$ breeding lies in the inventory of fissionable material.

section: III. COMPARISON OF PLUTONIUM AND U233 BREEDING
text: This inventory is much larger for plutonium breeders than for $U^{233}$ breeders.

section: III. COMPARISON OF PLUTONIUM AND U233 BREEDING
text: The large inventory is mainly a consequence of basic physical facts: because of the energy dependence of the $\eta$ of $\mathsf{Pu}^{239}$ , plutonium breeders have to operate at high neutron energies where the cross sections are small and where it takes many plutonium atoms to catch a neutron with sufficient probability before it escapes or slows down.

section: III. COMPARISON OF PLUTONIUM AND U233 BREEDING
text: The specific power of the Enrico Fermi Fast Breeder Reactor is $149\mathrm{kw / kg}$ of total inventory of fissionable material, or approximately $1\mathrm{kw / kg}$ of natural uranium (assuming that essentially all $\mathbf{U}^{235}$ contained in natural uranium could be used in the reactor).

text: S. and Canadian uranium resources recoverable at present prices are, according to Arnold and Ullmann, 550,000 tons, which would allow the production of 550,000 Mw (thermal), or $1.6\times 10^{16}$ Btu/year.

text: A strong case can be made for parallel development of the plutonium and $\mathbf{U}^{233}$ breeding cycles.

section: III. COMPARISON OF PLUTONIUM AND U233 BREEDING
text: More important, the optimum development might very well involve a start with a low-inventory, short-doubling-time $\mathbf{U}^{233}$ breeder which would allow, with a limited supply of fissionable material, to produce a substantial amount of power and a substantial yearly increase in the power production.

text: With the fissionable material supply increased by these breeders, high inventory plutonium breeders could be put into operation in order to tap the $\mathbf{U}^{238}$ supply.
